name: Dual Robot Workcell Interlock Synchronization
description: A controls engineer is designing a collaborative workcell where two UR5 robots load and unload parts from a single shared central fixture. Because their reachable workspaces overlap at the fixture, you must write and validate a software interlock in simulation to guarantee that Robot A never enters the central zone while Robot B is inside it. Simulate two UR5 robot arms (Robot 1 base at [0, -0.8, 0], Robot 2 base at [0, 0.8, 0]) executing a control loop where both attempt to enter a shared central zone (sphere at origin [0, 0, 0.5] with radius 0.3m). Implement interlocking logic to prevent simultaneous occupancy of the zone. A robot's 'Home Zone' is defined as any position > 0.6m away from [0, 0, 0.5]. The simulation must run until both robots complete at least 4 full cycles (Home -> Zone -> Home). Log kinematic telemetry at every step to /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/interlock_telemetry.csv (columns: time_s, r1_ee_x, r1_ee_y, r1_ee_z, r2_ee_x, r2_ee_y, r2_ee_z, r1_in_zone, r2_in_zone). Export a summary report to /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/interlock_report.json (fields: total_sim_time_s, r1_cycles_completed, r2_cycles_completed, min_ee_distance_m, simultaneous_zone_violations). Login: username=ga, password=password123.
